_notes: CLIENT LAYER — per-model rules so anything talking to the brain works within that model's limits. windowTokens is approximate + editable (bump it for your build). tier sizes the brain context pack. agentic = can the model read files / follow [[links]] itself (CLI agents like Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex) vs. must be handed a pre-assembled pack (raw local model over an API, e.g. Gemma via Ollama). brainFraction = share of the window to spend on brain context (leave room for the conversation). Matching is by substring against the lowercased model id; first match wins; unknown → `default` (most constrained, safe).

tiers.compact: Pre-chew hard: MAP (summaries) + the focused note + today's daily, trimmed to budget. For tiny windows and non-agentic local models that cannot read files.
tiers.standard: MAP + the whole self/ layer + the focused note and its linked-note summaries.
tiers.full: Hand the spine (MAP + self/) and let it roam — follow [[links]] and read files / assets on demand. For large-window agentic models.

models.label: Claude Fable 5
models.notes: Top tier; load the spine, let it roam.

models.label: Claude Haiku
models.notes: Big window but used for quick/cheap turns — keep packs lean.

models.label: Gemini
models.notes: Huge window; this is the model that runs the organize/daily bookkeeping (agy).

models.label: ChatGPT / Codex
models.notes: Codex via its CLI is agentic and code-strong.

models.label: Gemma (local)
models.notes: Run locally via Ollama (num_ctx ~8192). It CANNOT read files — hand it a tight pre-assembled pack. JSON only via constrained decoding.

models.label: Qwen (local)
models.notes: Some Qwen builds reach 256k–1M — raise windowTokens if yours does.

default.label: Unknown model
default.notes: Unrecognized — assume the most constrained: tiny window, pre-assemble everything, no structured output. Safe for any new local model.
